hawkish_phrases: domestic price and wage pressures have been stronger than expected, suggesting risks of greater persistence in underlying inflation
If there were to be evidence of more persistent pressures, then further tightening in monetary policy would be required
the Committee continued to judge that the risks to inflation were skewed significantly to the upside
services inflation had surprised to the upside, rising to a 30-year high of 6.8%
Annual private sector regular Average Weekly Earnings growth had risen to a little over 7% in the three months to November, 0.7 percentage points above the November Report projection
dovish_phrases: The real economy remained weak, as a result of falling real incomes and the tightening in financial conditions over the past year
the lags in the effects of monetary policy meant that sizeable impacts from past rate increases were still to come through
current setting of Bank Rate would be likely to reduce inflation to well below target in the medium term
Wholesale gas prices have fallen recently and global supply chain disruption appears to have eased
There were continuing signs that the downturn was affecting the labour market, especially in more forward-looking indicators
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to increase Bank Rate by 0.5 percentage points to 4%, with strong emphasis on persistent domestic wage and price pressures despite two members preferring to hold at 3.5%, and explicit forward guidance that further tightening would be required if pressures persist.
